reasoning: The knowledge cutoff is April 25, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025—a very short window of about two months (67 days). Changes to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) are relatively infrequent; historically, there have been around 50–60 changes in roughly 130 years (about once every 2+ years on average). While changes can cluster, the DJIA just underwent a restructuring in November 2024 (adding Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams) and February 2024 (adding Amazon). It is uncommon for index providers to alter the composition again so soon after a rebalance without a forcing event like a huge corporate action (e.g. going private, monumental stock split affecting price weightings). None of the 30 companies on the current list are in imminent danger of being acquired, and despite recent price volatility due to tariffs (with Nike and Caterpillar falling heavily in early April 2025), their share prices and market positions remain far above the historic thresholds that prompt ejection (such as Intel or Walgreens dropping to the low $20s and suffering structural core business decline). The baseline probability of a change occurring in any given 2-month period is less than 10%, and given the recent rebalancing, an immediate subsequent change appears even less likely. Therefore, I assign a 6% probability to an official announcement of a DJIA component removal before July 1, 2025.

recent news or announcements about companies being dropped from the Dow Jones index 2025
